metadata.first_question_preview: In genetic algorithms, the choice of a specific representation
metadata.assignment_note: Inferred from segment count/content because translated all-in-one TXT contains no filename headers.

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, the choice of a specific representation

answers.a: Is independent of the problem to be solved
answers.b: Depends on the problem to be solved
answers.d: Is impossible, as only real number representation is used
answers.e: Influences the quality of the provided solution.

questions.question: Within an evolutionary algorithm, the termination condition:

answers.a: Is mandatory
answers.c: Obligatorily includes, directly or indirectly, the control of the number of iterations (the number of generated populations)
answers.e: Is used only within GA, not within ES.

questions.question: Within the 2-member evolutionary strategy (ES-2M):

answers.a: An initial population is randomly generated from the normal distribution
answers.b: At every point in the algorithm's evolution, only a single solution candidate is maintained
answers.d: The calculation of a new term is performed via Gaussian mutation for every component of the current vector
answers.g: The calculation of a new term is performed via Gaussian mutation for a randomly selected component of the current vector
answers.h: It is intended to increase the average quality of the current population
answers.i: Obtaining a global optimum is guaranteed
answers.j: At the beginning of each iteration, the size of the current population is tested
answers.k:
Descendants are created, obtained from the current population through local recombination.
Correct: a, b, d, e, f, g, h, j

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, representation via integer strings

answers.a: Is preferable for optimization problems
answers.b: Cannot be used in the case of ordinal attributes
answers.c: Is preferable when more than two distinct values are possible for each gene
answers.d: Is not used in genetic algorithms
answers.e: Is merely an implementation exercise, not being necessary

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, the parent selection operation

answers.a: Is generally used in a non-deterministic manner
answers.b: Is merely an implementation exercise, not being necessary
answers.e: Is used depending on the chromosomal representation

questions.question: In an evolutionary algorithm, the evaluation function:

answers.a: is chosen depending on the population size
answers.b: is defined on the one-dimensional space
answers.e: estimates the adaptation level of the individual

questions.question: Let X=[ 6 1 8 10 5 7 12 43 9 3 4 2 ] be a sequence of integers. Following the application of the fluaj mutation, the following mutated variant can be obtained:
Genetic Algorithms

answers.a: Are deterministic search methods in the solution space
answers.c: Solve exclusively problems defined on continuous spaces
answers.d: Use at every moment in time a solution variant modified by normal perturbation
answers.e: Are stochastic search techniques based on populations

questions.question: The ES2M algorithm:

answers.a: It is the primary self-adaptive algorithm
answers.b: It is not an evolutionary algorithm
answers.d: It is not a self-adaptive algorithm

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm, the survivor selection operation:

answers.a: Is applied to the current population
answers.b: In some variants requires the calculation of a selection probability distribution
answers.d: Selects the next generation from the individuals resulting from the mutation operation
answers.e: The selected individuals are always feasible
answers.g: Ensures the perpetuation of the individual with maximum quality from the current population
answers.h: Leads to an increase in the average quality of the current population
answers.i: Guarantees obtaining a generation with superior average quality, if it uses age-based selection
answers.j: Is applied at the beginning of each iteration
answers.k: Is applied to the offspring obtained from the current population
answers.l: Is applied to the current population and to the offspring obtained from the current population.

questions.question: The types of problems that can be solved based on evolutionary computation are:

answers.a: Optimization problems
answers.g: Dynamic allocation of data in computer memory

questions.question: Let X=[ 6 1 8 10 5 7 9 3 4 2 ] be a permutation. Following the application of the inversion mutation for positions (2,7), the following mutated variant Y can be obtained:

Let the permutations be x=[10 2391 7648 5] and y=[5 4 7 2 1 9 3 6 8 10], a child chromosome resulting from PMX is c=[ 5 2 3 9 1 4 7 6 8 10]. Then the order of parent consideration and the crossover points are, respectively:
Which of the following methods can be used within memetic algorithms to improve the information within a population:

answers.a: recursion

questions.question: Decoding must be performed:

answers.a: None of the other variants
answers.b: Is not necessary in genetic algorithms
answers.c: Does not depend on the chosen representation
answers.d: Mandatory after extracting the best individual from the final population

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, representation via permutations

answers.a: Does not allow the use of recombination operators
answers.b: Does not allow the use of mutation operators
answers.e: Does not allow more than two genes with the same value in a chromosome

questions.question: Within an evolutionary algorithm, the initial population:

answers.a: Is generated before the actual evolution starts
answers.b: Is generated after every evolutionary cycle (which involves performing selection, recombination, mutation, and evaluation operations)
answers.c: Is generated at the beginning of every evolutionary cycle
answers.e: Is generated using the uniform probability distribution
answers.f: Is generated using the normal probability distribution
answers.g: Is set to the empty set.

questions.question: Let X=[ 61 810579342] be a permutation. Following the application of the swap mutation for positions (2,7), the following mutated variant Y can be obtained:
Within a genetic algorithm, the recombination operation:

answers.a: Is performed only once during a genetic algorithm
answers.b: Is performed immediately after each parent selection stage
answers.c: In general, the recombination probability does not matter in solving problems using genetic algorithms
answers.d: Is performed immediately before each mutation procedure
answers.e: Is performed immediately before each parent selection stage
answers.g: Is used only in unconstrained problems
answers.h: Is used with a high probability
answers.i: Is used with a low probability
answers.j: Is performed only once, after the first parent selection stage.

questions.question: Evolutionary computation is inspired by:

answers.a: Natural biological evolution

questions.question: Let the following permutation type chromosome be: {7, 1, 3,10, 8,15,11, 5,4,1,13, 2, 9}. Following the application of the swap mutation operator, the chromosome obtained is: {7,14,13, 1, 2, 8, 6, 3,11, 5,10, 4,9}. The two positions used for the swap are:

answers.a: 1 and 14

questions.question: Which of the following operators can be used in a genetic algorithm that uses representation via real number strings?

answers.a: Negation

questions.correct_raw: e, f, n, o, p, r, s, t

questions.question: Let X=[ 6 1 8 10 5 7 9 3 4 2 ] and Y=[ 9 8 7 3 6 1 5 10 4 2] be permutations. Which offspring are generated by using the CX operator?
Within an evolutionary strategies algorithm class, the chromosome representation:

answers.a: Can be any of: binary strings, integer strings, real number strings, permutations
answers.b: Can only be of type integer strings or real number strings
answers.c: Can only be of type real number strings
answers.d: Does not influence the type of recombination used (discrete / intermediate)
answers.e: Is chosen according to the problem being solved
answers.f: Influences the type of recombination used
answers.g: Has influence over the selection mechanism for the next generation
answers.h: Contains both the description of the candidate individual and the parameters controlling its evolution.

questions.question: Within an evolutionary strategies algorithm class, the mutation operation:

answers.a: Is used only in problems with constraints
answers.b: Is performed immediately once an offspring population is available
answers.c: Selects on average half the individuals for mutation
answers.d: Uses two distinct multisets of offspring
answers.f: Is performed only once, after the first stage of generating a population
answers.g: Is carried out with a small probability
answers.h: Is performed only once during an algorithm run

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm that maximizes a function (fitness):

answers.a: The selection of the next generation is mandatorily based on age
answers.c: Mutation is carried out with a small probability
answers.d: The use of FPS selection is not recommended
answers.e: The offspring multiset is without duplicates
answers.f: The selection of survivors is determined by quality and/or the age factor
answers.h: Determining a global maximum point is pursued
answers.i: The chromosomal structure differs if the function is translated.

questions.question: Given the following two permutation-type chromosomes: {6, 3, 7, 8, 5, 1, 2, 4, 9 } and {7, 2, 8, 5, 6, 9, 4, 1, 3}. Applying the PMX recombination operator, with positions 3 and 6, the resulting offspring are:
The components of evolutionary algorithms are:

answers.a: Representation
answers.k: Establishing the genetic diversity of the population
answers.l: The mechanism for replacing the current population

questions.correct_raw: a, c, e, g, j, l, n, o

questions.question: The Hillclimbing algorithm:

answers.a: Is applied to a single point in the search space
answers.b: Application can be repeated for multiple points to increase performance
answers.f: Calculations end when the system temperature becomes zero
answers.g: Usually finds a local optimum point
answers.h: Is used only for representation with real number strings.

questions.question: In a genetic algorithm, the initial population:

answers.a: Is generated until a local optimum is reached
answers.c: Undergoes mutation with a relatively small probability
answers.d: Must be modified at every iteration
answers.f: Is formed by the individuals that pass to the next generation
answers.g: Is generated using the uniform probability distribution.
